title: LLMs are inherently low IQ when it comes to doing actions. Though engineering we can help. But be cautious using it for trading.
author: Traditional_Ear5237
selftext:
A bit of an insight from someone who has been building these systems for quite a while. Inherently LLMs tend to produce natural language rather than structured json output needed for it to do work like call tools and execute real commands. If you are wiring one up for trading - make sure you isolate the context in a smart way where you are not introducing the incentive for it to spit out long tests instead of actions like placing a trade, managing your stop loss levels and etc. You will often see that it will report to you that it has done something when in reality it has not. It is a common pitfall of LLMs and since this community cares a lot about making money I thought I would leave this information here. There are 2 industries from the top of my head where AI mistakes are always high stakes (I rhymed that one didn't I? :D) Given that trading = money - it is high stakes you want to ensure execution. Build modular design where each part is only responsible for like 1 or 2 actions. The more you load each node with action possibilities the less it will be able to effectively execute. Also add at least some actor-critic system -> this will improve execution prudence. And don't give your systems too much capital. LLM on its own is a very overfitted network. And LLMs suck at numbers. The main danger of AI + Trading = Overfitting. Data is noisy and non stationary. LLMs do seem to posses some general intelligence, but be cautious, if the LLM knows what asset you are trading it is worse than if it does not because you are not introducing the bias where it will search related vectorized tokens and present them as reasoning where in reality it is just past data verbalized.

In either case, if you see it performing very well, do not jump and give it 10x more money. You might have caught an uptrend or a downtrend. Verify over longer time periods. I suggest to NOT BACKTEST LLM BASED STRATEGIES. Why? Because first of all it is costly to invoke the model 10k times to test. And 2nd -> it knows all the past information so your back tests are inherently overfitted.

Cheers!
